item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love that this is a more capable Pesterbot, but it doesn't want to kill or harm anyone, it is just a little prankster that is messing with a specific researcher. I love this little bastard!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a fun read. I loved how you tied it all back to the rubix cube in the end.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first I thought this was pretty derivative of wondertainment SCPs but I’m so glad I stuck with it

The pay off for the inclusion of the Rubik’s cube is delightful

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall enjoyable. :)

I suggest adding "cuts along the [entire] surface". It currently isn't clear how extensive the damage/dangerous-surface is.

I suggest adding a sentence or two about how it was acquired.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I first read through this article when it was a draft. I really hoped that it would get published because I really loved the concept. Good job on this.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a stray "Researcher Coleman" in the 2nd "favorites" test when it was Arelli pretending to be DeVorace

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed this SCP, it's simple yet really fun. Just an SCP that is focused on annoying Arelli, showing affection for Coleman cause it annoys Arelli? The overall narrative is satisfying, great work.

+1

item.title: Improvement
item.content:encoded:
The rubik cube at the end does not have a proper reward for the robot, since solving it does not result in a prank type activity, so it's massively out of character(ruining the punchline).

The article could be massively improved if head researcher Arelli was the one being contained by a fundation loyal AI, and the robot rewrote Arelli's SCP entry and various computer files to make it look like the robot is the SCP (to protect Arelli from O5, who had to take amnestics about it for —- reasons).

P.S.: HONK!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A Robot being a bitch. Simple, yet effective +1

item.title: Re: nitpicks
item.content:encoded:
The SPaG errors you mentioned should be fixed, but there are probably a few more lurking around that I missed.

SCP-5590 will blatantly lie about not hating Head Researcher Arelli whenever possible.

Yes, this is jumping to conclusions. My intention was to show Arelli's frustration by having him jump to conclusions, but I see that it isn't clear enough that Arelli wrote that note. I've added the line "SCP-5590 doesn't lie about hating you. Chances are it doesn't even know who you are." To Site Director Hoff's email, which will (hopefully) make it more clear that Arelli wrote that line.

item.title: wow
item.content:encoded:
As I was reading, I noticed some minor issues with spag and it was slowly building towards something, but I didn't know what. Then Test #5 comes around the corner…dang! Then Test #6 is even worse! I feel kind of bad for the robot, but still don't know why he is such an ass to Arelli. But of course, it attempts to be a non-violent nuisance as much as possible.

The pacing overall was quite good and the execution of the story was great.

+1

nitpicks
close

SCP-5590 drew a portrait of Researcher Coleman next to an unidentified, crudely drawn indivigual.

individual

The height difference between Researcher Coleman and the infivigual loosely matches

Same as previous. This can't be intentional, right?

SCP-5590 has exhibited a similar reaction in subsequent tests with all other staff members (except for Head Researcher Arelli).

I don't think the parentheses are permissible here. The fact that the robot doesn't do this to only Arelli should be included with the rest of the sentence.

SCP-5590 will blatantly lie about now hating Head Researcher Arelli whenever possible.

I don't know that they can make the conclusion that the robot hates Arelli, maybe that the robot dislikes Arelli and isn't willing to speak more about the issue. There isn't any evidence so far that the robot tries to seriously hurt Arelli.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the first article I felt was good enough to publish. If you're reading this, chances are it isn't getting deleted, which is pretty neat.

+ some stuff you probably won't care about
- thanks for reading anyway

SCP-5031 is one of my favorite articles on the Wiki and was a huge inspiration for this. In the first draft of this article, Head Researcher Arelli even says this in an interview to 5590:

I want to know what makes you tick, because once I find out how you keep learning new ways to make me miserable I can lock you in a box and pretend you never existed.

In a sense, 5590 is the polar opposite to 5031. 5031 is locked up and neglected because it looks like a big scary murder monster, when in fact it just needed some love and attention. Arelli is forced to give 5590 attention when he would be much better off locking it up and neglecting it.

- thanks for reading anyway

Big thanks to RockTeethMothEyes, cybersqyd, PlaguePJP, Roget, RL224, YellowCorp, chiifu and Alfadillo Gonadicus for the critique.

Image Credit: Image taken from Wikimedia Commons and edited by me.
